title: kubectl port-forward "Connection reset by peer"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi,
I am using podman on Fedora 35.
I am able to run `podman run -p 12345:80 nginx` and from the host:

```console
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:12345 
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<title>Welcome to nginx!</title>
<style>
...
```

Instead if I start a container (`-p 8080:8080`) with `kubectl` installed and configured to connect to a GKE cluster, if I run inside the container:

```console
$ kubectl run nginx --image nginx --port 80 
$ kubectl port-forward nginx 8080:80
``` 

From inside the container:

```console
$ curl 127.0.0.1:8080
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<title>Welcome to nginx!</title>
<style>
...
```

But from the host:

```console
$ curl -v http://127.0.0.1:8080 
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.79.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
* Closing connection 0
curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 21:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body: How is this a Podman issue and not a kubelet issue?
Does kubectl listens on localhost only? Can you check with `ss -tulpn` in the container?

```console
$ ss -tulpn
Netid         State          Recv-Q         Send-Q                 Local Address:Port                 Peer Address:Port        Process                                   
tcp           LISTEN         0              128                        127.0.0.1:8080                      0.0.0.0:*            users:(("kubectl",pid=1627,fd=8))        
tcp           LISTEN         0              128                            [::1]:8080                         [::]:*            users:(("kubectl",pid=1627,fd=9))        
```

Yes you are right.
`kubectl port-forward nginx 8080:80  --address 0.0.0.0 ` works:

```console
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:20615
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<title>Welcome to nginx!</title>
<style>
```

Sorry for wasting your time.

> --address 0.0.0.0

This does not work for me. I still constantly see this where others on my team do not. 

`
Handling connection for 7687
Handling connection for 7687
Handling connection for 7687
E0920 11:53:24.740012   68846 portforward.go:406] an error occurred forwarding 7687 -> 7687: error forwarding port 7687 to pod b87b23dd492d247bc96b403e326d91747bb91cb84e0ba2bc76563f96729679dd, uid : failed to execute portforward in network namespace "/var/run/netns/cni-1f5614ee-6e10-a9fb-88fd-2b4a9ef8ee9f": read tcp4 127.0.0.1:46540->127.0.0.1:7687: read: connection reset by peer
E0920 11:53:24.742390   68846 portforward.go:234] lost connection to pod
`
